title: [CI:DOCS] Restore man page cross-checker
body:
Somewhere in the CIv2 migration we lost the man page vs --help
cross-checker. Add it back, by adding it into the man-page-check
Makefile target; this is part of 'make validate', which is run
in CI even on CI:DOCS PRs.

As happens when CI doesn't run, things broke. Man pages got out
of sync with --help. This PR:

 1) Fixes hack/xref-helpmsgs-manpages to deal with the new
    "Options" (instead of "Flags") form of podman help. #8034
    did part of that, but one of my review comments was
    accidentally left out.

 2) Fixes hack/xref-helpmsgs-manpages to deal with the new
    option syntax in man pages, post- #8292, in which each
    option is preceded by four hashes so as to make them
    HTML <h4> elements with named anchors.

 3) Fixes man pages that #8292 accidentally missed.

Fixes: #8296

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

THIS IS GOING TO FAIL CI. At least I hope so. I've deliberately introduced two errors, because I want to make sure that the validation check will catch them. ("I want to see a negative before I see a positive." "What's that going to prove?" "Indulge me.")

I would've liked to make this a CI-colon-DOCS PR but cannot, because CI-colon-DOCS is currently a NOP: it doesn't even check docs. This is kind of bad, and will need to be fixed, but it's left as an exercise for another reader. (See #8297)

Followup: as expected (and desired), initial commit [failed to validate](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5457930671620096?command=main#L104). After pushing with corrected `.md` files, it [passed](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/4857872737632256). This is ready for review.
Should this be CI:DOCS? Does CI:DOCS run your check?
@mheon see comment 1. CI:DOCS is currently broken.
@edsantiago pls rebase on the latest #8297 which includes (verified) working CI:DOCS support.
@cevich sorry, #8297 includes an incorrect (and soon-to-be-broken) incantation in `runner.sh`. This PR solves the `xref-manpages` problem in a different, better way.

Rebased on master, which now includes #8297, which now means I can use CI-colon-DOCS. Confirmed that even with CI-colon-docs, [validate runs, and runs the xref script, and passes](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5162292536934400?command=main#L104). Merge me please.
